title: Headers and source separation deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific deterministic MCQ and fill-gap evidence for Headers and source separation; human review required.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Headers and source separation: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Moving any function definition into a header is safe because include guards prevent duplicate definitions at link time.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Include guards prevent repeated text in one translation unit; cross-unit definitions must still satisfy inline, template, or one-definition rules.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Include guards prevent repeated text in one translation unit; cross-unit definitions must still satisfy inline, template, or one-definition rules.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Include guards prevent repeated text in one translation unit; cross-unit definitions must still satisfy inline, template, or one-definition rules.
Moving any function definition into a header is safe because include guards prevent duplicate definitions at link time.

items.title: Headers and source separation: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing CPP checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: Moving any function definition into a header is safe because include guards prevent duplicate definitions at link time.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'one-definition rules'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Include guards prevent repeated text in one translation unit; cross-unit definitions must still satisfy inline, template, or one-definition rules.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact CPP token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.
